4c8b2873-395b-4840-b269-1fb6f70d7a5c.title: Assemble ordered chain and verify one adjacency pair
4c8b2873-395b-4840-b269-1fb6f70d7a5c.goal: Merge visited_url outputs from the visits into an ordered chain (starting with Main Page and following the chosen hops). Produce a final ordered list of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Verify one adjacency pair near the end by providing the HTML snippet from the earlier page that contains the anchor to the later page (showing that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).

551c46a7-7b68-4f7f-b8ea-6a4f7ab05eb2.title: Merge: Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).
551c46a7-7b68-4f7f-b8ea-6a4f7ab05eb2.goal: Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).

final_output.final_deliverable: Ordered chain of Wikipedia URLs (start -> end): 1) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page 2) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:WhatLinksHere/Main_Page 3) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Incoming_links 4) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Internal_links Evidence and provenance (verbatim quotes from the visit data, with citations): Step 1 -> Step 2 (Main Page -> Special:WhatLinksHere/Main_Page) - Claim: The Main Page includes (or lists) a link to Special:WhatLinksHere/Main_Page. - Evidence: The visit output for the Main Page includes the extracted links array showing the exact URL. Verbatim from the merged/visit data showing that outgoing link (quote from the structured visit data produced during the Main Page visit): "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:WhatLinksHere/Main_Page" (from the Main Page visit links list) — see the Main Page visit data: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page (The Main Page visit output included a links array containing: "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:WhatLinksHere/Main_Page".) Source citation: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page Verbatim quote from the RETRIEVED/MERGED visit output (shows the exact URL in the page's extracted links list): "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:WhatLinksHere/Main_Page" Note: The raw page text captured for Main Page did not contain the literal HTML anchor tags in the provided raw-text extract; the structured visit output (links list) contains the explicit target URL above. Step 2 -> Step 3 (Special:WhatLinksHere/Main_Page -> Incoming_links) - Claim: The Special:WhatLinksHere/Main_Page page contains or lists the Incoming_links page. - Evidence (verbatim from the RAW VISIT CONTENT for the Special:WhatLinksHere/Main_Page visit): "=== https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Incoming_links ===" (this header appears in the Special:WhatLinksHere/Main_Page visit raw content) — see: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:WhatLinksHere/Main_Page Source citation: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:WhatLinksHere/Main_Page Additional structured-visit evidence: the Special:WhatLinksHere/Main_Page visit's links array included the Incoming_links page URL: "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Incoming_links" (present in the visit output). Step 3 -> Step 4 (Incoming_links -> Internal_links) - Claim: The Incoming_links page links to (or references) the Internal_links page (the "Internal links" article). - Evidence (verbatim from the RAW VISIT CONTENT for the Incoming_links page): "Some other words for\nbacklink\nare\nincoming link, inbound link, inlink, inward link, and citation.\nBacklinks and search engines\nMain article:\nLink building\nSearch engines\noften use the number of backlinks ...\nSee also\nLink farm\u2013 Group of websites that link to each others\nLinkback\u2013 Method of notifications for web authors\nMethods of website linking\u2013 Efforts to increase the number of high-quality links pointing to a website\nInternal links\u2013 Hyperlinks to resources in the same domain or website" (verbatim excerpt from the Incoming_links raw content) The exact phrase in the Incoming_links raw content that names the Internal-links article is: "Internal links 6 Hyperlinks to resources in the same domain or website" Source citation: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Incoming_links Also, the Incoming_links visit's links list (structured output) includes the exact URL "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Internal_links", confirming the Incoming_links page points to or references the Internal_links page. Verified adjacency pair (explicit verification near the end of the chain): - Verified pair chosen: index 3 -> 4 in the chain (Incoming_links -> Internal_links) - From URL (index): 3 from_url: "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Incoming_links" - To URL (index): 4 to_url: "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Internal_links" - Verification evidence (what was requested): * The RAW visit content for Incoming_links contains the visible reference text naming the Internal-links article. Verbatim excerpt from that raw page content: "Internal links 6 Hyperlinks to resources in the same domain or website" (from https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Incoming_links) * The structured visit output (links array) for the Incoming_links page includes the exact target URL string: "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Internal_links" (present in the Incoming_links page's extracted links list) - Important note about HTML: the provided RAW VISIT CONTENT (the textual extracts in the visit outputs supplied to me) does not include the original HTML anchor tag snippet (for example, an exact ... fragment). That precise HTML anchor snippet was not present in the raw-text extracts provided. Therefore I cannot quote an actual HTML snippet from the page. Instead I verified the adjacency using the verbatim textual evidence (the page's "See also" / heading text naming the target) and the visit's extracted links list (which contains the exact target URL). If you require the literal HTML anchor snippet, that would require capturing the page's HTML source; the supplied visit-text extracts do not contain that HTML anchor markup. Notes on how links were chosen (brief): - Starting point was the required start URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page. - The second hop used a special-page link discovered among the Main Page's outgoing links: Special:WhatLinksHere/Main_Page (an internal Special page that lists pages that link to the Main Page). This was selected because it was clearly present in the Main Page's outgoing links array in the visit output. - Next, from Special:WhatLinksHere/Main_Page I followed the visible listed subpage Incoming_links (this appeared as a named section/header and in the page's links list), and from Incoming_links I followed its "See also" reference to Internal links (the Internal_links article), yielding a chain of four Wikipedia URLs. Full citations for each page in the chain (these are the pages I visited / used as sources): - https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page - https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:WhatLinksHere/Main_Page - https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Incoming_links - https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Internal_links What evidence is present vs. missing (explicit): - Present: the visit outputs include the exact target URLs in structured "links" arrays for the visited pages; the RAW page text extracts include the headings and "See also" lines that name the linked pages. I have quoted those verbatim above. - Missing: the literal HTML anchor () snippet containing the href attribute as HTML was not included in the provided raw-text visit extracts, so I cannot present an actual HTML anchor snippet. I have therefore supplied the verbatim textual lines that demonstrate the link's presence and the extracted links-array entries that contain the exact URL. If you want, I can (a) attempt to re-visit the specific pages and request the HTML source so I can extract the exact anchor snippet for the verified adjacency, or (b) produce a different chain and verify a different adjacency pair where the provided raw extract includes the HTML anchor (if available).
final_output.action_summary: Chain (4 pages): 1) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page -> 2) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:WhatLinksHere/Main_Page -> 3) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Incoming_links -> 4) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Internal_links. Verified adjacency (Incoming_links -> Internal_links) with verbatim evidence from the Incoming_links raw content: "Internal links 		6 Hyperlinks to resources in the same domain or website" and the Incoming_links visit's extracted links list containing "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Internal_links". Note: the literal HTML <a href=...> anchor snippet was not present in the provided raw-text extracts, so the HTML anchor could not be quoted; instead textual 'See also' text and the links-array entry were used to prove the adjacency. Citations: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:WhatLinksHere/Main_Page, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Incoming_links, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Internal_links.
